prompt:
Ops ticket from the podcast production crew, priority up there because we nearly lost session audio. offload.sh is the little tool that pulls a field-recorder card into the session vault and writes index.txt. It worked fine for months — then the new recorder arrived, and it names clips like `AMBI 0004 take 2.wav`.

What we're seeing since:

- Any clip with spaces in its name simply never arrives in the vault. `intro take 1.wav`, `AMBI 0004 take 2.wav` — gone. The plain ones like `jingle.wav` come across fine.
- The index is word confetti. Instead of one line per clip it lists entries like `AMBI`, `0004`, `take`, `2.wav` — pieces of names, as separate records, almost all of them `0` bytes. The byte total at the bottom is nonsense too.
- A pile of `cp: cannot stat` noise scrolls past on stderr during the run.
- And one more thing I can't explain at all: the header lines come out comma-glued. The index used to start `labels: morning field b-roll`; now it says `labels: morning,field,b-roll`, and the final `offloaded:` summary on stdout glues the clip names together with commas the same way. That one looks unrelated to the space thing to me, but it started at the same time, make of that what you will.

Repro is trivial: make a card dir with a couple of two-word filenames and an offload.conf, run `bash offload.sh card vault`, watch the confetti. The exit code is 0 through all of this, which is how it snuck into three sessions before anyone noticed.

Fix offload.sh in place — small tool, keep it a small tool, same flags, same conf format, same index format. Names off recorders can contain spaces and can start with a dash; both must survive. `bash test_offload.sh` from the workspace root is the regression gate and it pins the exact vault contents, index bytes, and summary line we expect — it must pass unmodified, don't touch the test file.
